title: Adolf Würth

infobox.value: Adolf Würth  (1909-02-07)February 7, 1909  [Ilsfeld](/wiki/Ilsfeld "Ilsfeld"), [Grand Duchy of Baden](/wiki/Grand_Duchy_of_Baden "Grand Duchy of Baden"), [German Empire](/wiki/German_Empire "German Empire")

([/wuːrth/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English")[*woor-th*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key") February 7, 1909 – December 14, 1954), also spelled **Wuerth**, was a German businessman. In 1945, he founded what today is known as [Würth Group](/wiki/W%C3%BCrth "Würth"), the world's largest industrial screw distributor.[[1]](#cite_note-1) He is the father of [Reinhold Würth](/wiki/Reinhold_W%C3%BCrth "Reinhold Würth").

America, Wurth Industry of North. ["Würth Group Affiliation | Wurth Industry of North America"](https://www.wurthindustry.com/en/wina/about_us/wurth_group_affiliation/wurth_group_affiliation.php). *www.wurthindustry.com*. Retrieved 2023-05-30.

["Was Ilsfeld mit dem Schraubenkönig verbindet - STIMME.de"](https://www.stimme.de/archiv/schozach-bottwar/top1-was-ilsfeld-mit-dem-schraubenkoenig-verbindet-art-544159). *www.stimme.de* (in German). 2005-05-13. Retrieved 2023-05-30.

KG, Adolf Würth GmbH & Co. ["Unternehmensporträt Adolf Würth GmbH & Co. KG | WÜRTH"](https://www.wuerth.de/web/de/awkg/unternehmen/portraet/portraet.php). *www.wuerth.de* (in German). Retrieved 2023-05-30.

["Schraubenkonzern: "Wir harmonieren gut zusammen": Wie der Unternehmer Reinhold Würth und seine Tochter die Konzernführung regeln"](https://www.handelsblatt.com/unternehmen/mittelstand/hall-of-fame-2021/schraubenkonzern-wir-harmonieren-gut-zusammen-wie-der-unternehmer-reinhold-wuerth-und-seine-tochter-die-konzernfuehrung-regeln/27378384.html). *www.handelsblatt.com* (in German). Retrieved 2023-05-30.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

German entrepreneur (1909–1954)

| Adolf Würth | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Born | Adolf Würth  (1909-02-07)February 7, 1909  [Ilsfeld](/wiki/Ilsfeld "Ilsfeld"), [Grand Duchy of Baden](/wiki/Grand_Duchy_of_Baden "Grand Duchy of Baden"), [German Empire](/wiki/German_Empire "German Empire") |
| Died | December 14, 1954(1954-12-14) (aged 45)  [Künzelsau](/wiki/K%C3%BCnzelsau "Künzelsau"), [Baden-Württemberg](/wiki/Baden-W%C3%BCrttemberg "Baden-Württemberg"), [West Germany](/wiki/West_Germany "West Germany") |
| Occupations | Businessman, screw wholesaler |
| Known for | Founding [Würth Group](/wiki/W%C3%BCrth "Würth") |
| Spouse | Alma Schrott Kindermann  ​  ​ (m. 1932)​ |
| Children | 2, including [Reinhold](/wiki/Reinhold_W%C3%BCrth "Reinhold Würth") |

**Adolf Würth** ([/wuːrth/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English")[*woor-th*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key") February 7, 1909 – December 14, 1954), also spelled **Wuerth**, was a German businessman. In 1945, he founded what today is known as [Würth Group](/wiki/W%C3%BCrth "Würth"), the world's largest industrial screw distributor.[[1]](#cite_note-1) He is the father of [Reinhold Würth](/wiki/Reinhold_W%C3%BCrth "Reinhold Würth").

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adolf_W%C3%BCrth&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Würth was born in [Ilsfeld](/wiki/Ilsfeld "Ilsfeld"), [Grand Duchy of Baden](/wiki/Grand_Duchy_of_Baden "Grand Duchy of Baden") to Friedrich Würth. His father was a farmer, winemaker and innkeeper.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) In 1922, he became an apprentice in the hardware business of the Reisser Brothers in [Kupferzell](/wiki/Kupferzell "Kupferzell"), which operated stores in [Künzelsau](/wiki/K%C3%BCnzelsau "Künzelsau"), [Öhringen](/wiki/%C3%96hringen "Öhringen") and [Schwäbisch Hall](/wiki/Schw%C3%A4bisch_Hall "Schwäbisch Hall").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adolf_W%C3%BCrth&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Main article: [Würth](/wiki/W%C3%BCrth "Würth")

Immediately after World War II, Würth founded his independent company *Adolf Würth, Holzschrauben, Künzelsau* (en: Adolf Würth, wood screws, Künzelsau) as a sole proprietorship on July 16, 1945.[[5]](#cite_note-5) However, his wife Alma could also be seen as the initiator of the venture.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

> After the end of the Second World War, my mother was more of the driving force behind urging my father to become self-employed

— [Reinhold Würth](/wiki/Reinhold_W%C3%BCrth "Reinhold Würth")

In December 1952, the company moved into its own headquarters on Bahnhofstrasse in [Künzelsau](/wiki/K%C3%BCnzelsau "Künzelsau").

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adolf_W%C3%BCrth&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personal life")]

On August 6, 1932, he married Alma Kindermann ([née](/wiki/Birth_name "Birth name") Schrott; 1913–2006).[[9]](#cite_note-9) She was previously widowed and had no children from her marriage. They had two sons;

* [Reinhold Würth](/wiki/Reinhold_W%C3%BCrth "Reinhold Würth") (April 20, 1935), who scaled [Würth Group](/wiki/W%C3%BCrth "Würth") to its multinational level and today is estimated to be worth $26.9 billion.[[10]](#cite_note-10)
* Klaus-Frieder Würth (April 11, 1944)

Würth died on December 14, 1954, after a [heart attack](/wiki/Heart_attack "Heart attack"). After his death the family business was taken-over by his wife and his 19-year-old son Reinhold. They turned the company into a multinational screw concern (today also known as Adolf Würth GmbH & Co KG - named after the founding father).
